gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The dotted nodule name from a `nodule X.Y` declaration (or the `// nodule:` marker)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `pub(crate)`: reused by [`crate::lib_index`] (the `docs/lib-index/` M-1004 extractor) rather"] # [doc = " than re-implemented — DRY, one nodule-name heuristic for both consumers."] pub (crate) fn nodule_name (src : & str) -> Option < String > { for line in src . lines () { let t = line . trim () ; if let Some (rest) = t . strip_prefix ("nodule ") { return Some (rest . trim () . trim_end_matches (['{' , ';' , ' ']) . to_owned ()) ; } } for line in src . lines () { let t = line . trim () ; if let Some (rest) = t . strip_prefix ("// nodule:") { return Some (rest . trim () . to_owned ()) ; } } None }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Extract `fn NAME(...) -> Ty` / `fn NAME(...) => Ty` signatures with their 1-based line numbers."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A parameter list may itself span several lines (e.g. `lib/std/text.myc::decode_two`, where the"] # [doc = " closing `)` + return type + body `=` land a few lines below `fn decode_two(`) — found while"] # [doc = " building the M-1004 lib-index extractor, which was truncating ~1.3% of `lib/`'s signatures to"] # [doc = " their bare `fn NAME(` opening line. Handled by joining lines until the body-introducing bare `=`"] # [doc = " is found (never open-ended: bounded by the file's own line count)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `pub(crate)`: reused by [`crate::lib_index`] (DRY — see [`nodule_name`])."] pub (crate) fn fn_signatures (src : & str) -> Vec < (String , u32) > { let lines : Vec < & str > = src . lines () . collect () ; let mut out = Vec :: new () ; let mut i = 0 ; while i < lines . len () { let t = lines [i] . trim () ; let Some (rest) = t . strip_prefix ("fn ") else { i += 1 ; continue ; } ; let start_line = (i + 1) as u32 ; let mut acc = rest . to_owned () ; let mut j = i ; while body_separator (& acc) == acc . as_str () && j + 1 < lines . len () { j += 1 ; acc . push (' ') ; acc . push_str (lines [j] . trim ()) ; } let sig = clean_join_spacing (body_separator (& acc) . trim ()) ; out . push ((format ! ("fn {sig}") , start_line)) ; i = j + 1 ; } out }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Undo the cosmetic artifact of joining indented continuation lines with a single space: a"] # [doc = " multi-line parameter list's own opening `(`/closing `)` picks up a stray adjacent space (e.g."] # [doc = " `decode_two( b: Bytes, i: Binary{8} )` instead of `decode_two(b: Bytes, i: Binary{8})`). No"] # [doc = " real `.myc` signature uses this spacing (checked over every `lib/` file), so this is a safe,"] # [doc = " content-preserving cleanup, not a risk of eating meaningful whitespace."] fn clean_join_spacing (sig : & str) -> String { sig . replace ("( " , "(") . replace (" )" , ")") }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Split a `fn` line at its body-introducing bare `=`, keeping the return-type arrow `=>` intact."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Mycelium's real surface syntax (every `.myc` file under `lib/`) writes the return type with a"] # [doc = " **fat arrow**: `fn f(x: T) => U = <body>`. A naive `split_once('=')` (the original M-736 cut)"] # [doc = " finds the `=` **inside** `=>` first and truncates the signature there, silently dropping the"] # [doc = " return type for every real `.myc` file — an accuracy bug, not a cosmetic one (found while"] # [doc = " building the M-1004 `docs/lib-index/` extractor, which reuses this function). Fixed here (once,"] # [doc = " DRY) rather than duplicated with a workaround in the caller: scan for the first `=` that is"] # [doc = " **not** immediately followed by `>` (i.e. not part of `=>`); that is the body separator. A"] # [doc = " signature with no such separator (e.g. truncated input) returns the line unchanged."] fn body_separator (rest : & str) -> & str { let bytes = rest . as_bytes () ; for (idx , & b) in bytes . iter () . enumerate () { if b == b'=' && bytes . get (idx + 1) != Some (& b'>') { return & rest [.. idx] ; } } rest }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The contiguous `//` doc-comment block immediately above the `fn` at `fn_line` (1-based), over"] # [doc = " the already-split source `lines`. The scan walks backward, joining `//` comment lines into one"] # [doc = " summary, and stops at the first blank line, non-comment line, or header line (`// nodule…` /"] # [doc = " `// @key:` are metadata, not doc prose). Returns `None` when the `fn` has no preceding comment —"] # [doc = " an honest, explicit gap (never invented filler, G2). The text is taken verbatim from source, so"] # [doc = " it always traces to its provenance. Takes a `&[&str]` so the caller splits the source once."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `pub(crate)`: reused by [`crate::lib_index`] (DRY — see [`nodule_name`])."] pub (crate) fn preceding_doc (lines : & [& str] , fn_line : u32) -> Option < String > { if fn_line == 0 || (fn_line as usize) > lines . len () { return None ; } let mut idx = (fn_line as usize) - 1 ; let mut collected : Vec < String > = Vec :: new () ; while idx > 0 { idx -= 1 ; let t = lines [idx] . trim () ; let Some (rest) = t . strip_prefix ("//") else { break ; } ; let rest = rest . trim () ; if rest . is_empty () || rest . starts_with ('@') || rest . starts_with ("nodule") { break ; } collected . push (rest . to_owned ()) ; } if collected . is_empty () { return None ; } collected . reverse () ; if collected [0] . starts_with ('─') { return None ; } Some (collected . join (" ")) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The function name from a `fn NAME(...)` signature."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `pub(crate)`: reused by [`crate::lib_index`] (DRY — see [`nodule_name`])."] pub (crate) fn fn_name (sig : & str) -> Option < String > { let rest = sig . strip_prefix ("fn ") ? ; let name : String = rest . chars () . take_while (| c | c . is_alphanumeric () || * c == '_' || * c == '.') . collect () ; if name . is_empty () { None } else { Some (name) } }

# [doc = " `pub(crate)`: reused by [`crate::lib_index`] (DRY — see [`nodule_name`])."] pub (crate) fn path_stem (path : & str) -> String { let file = path . rsplit ('/') . next () . unwrap_or (path) ; file . rsplit_once ('.') . map_or (file , | (s , _) | s) . to_owned () }
